Uniface Applications

Uniface Applications

Uniface applications are typically mission-critical enterprise applications that use and maintain data stored in one or more databases, files, or other data resources. They may integrate with all kinds of non-Uniface applications, and they can be deployed on a wide variety of platforms.

Each Uniface application is a collection of runtime objects that run in a Uniface runtime environment, which consists of executables, application servers, connectors, and data sources. Uniface applications are based on a client/server architecture in which the user interface runs on the client and all other functionality runs on a server.

The type of application determines the kinds of runtime objects it requires and the runtime environment in which it is deployed. For example:

  • Web and mobile applications use dynamic server page components to provide the user interface, and they require a web server to host the application and communicate with Uniface application server.
  • Desktop applications use forms and global objects such as menus and panels to provide the user interface. They will require the Uniface runtime executable, and probably a Uniface application server to execute remote service components.

Application Objects

Uniface runtime objects are the deployable application components and resources, such as

  • User interface components for displaying data and handling user interactions.
  • Service components for performing business logic such as calculations, calculations, data access, or other data processing.
  • Libraries containing common code modules, messages, menus and other global objects.
  • Application shells for desktop applications and application servers.

For more information, see Uniface Runtime Objects.

Application Environment

A Uniface application typically runs in a distributed environment that includes some or all of the following executables, processes, and connectors:

  • The Uniface Runtime Engine for executing local components. For example, it may run in a browser to execute client-side code, in a Uniface executable to run desktop components, and in a Uniface Server to run server-side code and connect to data stores.
  • Uniface Server for executing remote components, such as services, reports, and server pages.
  • Uniface Router that directs component requests and messages to Uniface Servers.
  • Various drivers, known as connectors, for connecting Uniface to databases, operating systems, networks, and other technologies.
  • Third-party web server with servlet engine, such as Apache Tomcat or IBM WebSphere
  • One or more data stores, such as relational databases containing application data.

For more information, see Uniface Runtime Environment.

Application Data

Application data may be stored in relational databases, files, and other data stores. For more information, see DBMS Support.

Related Links
  • Deployment Models

    Uniface applications can be deployed according to a variety of deployment models, suitable for local, distributed, or internet access.

  • Deploying Applications

    Deploying a Uniface application entails installing the Uniface runtime servers in the appropriate locations, distributing the application runtime objects to the most effective locations in the infrastructure, and editing the configuration files so that the Uniface runtime environment can locate the required resources.
